description:
You are acting as a compliance analyst who must create an auditable checksum
    record for a policy file.

    1. A file already exists at  
       /home/user/documents/policy.txt  

    2. Calculate the SHA-256 checksum of the above file **exactly as it is on
       disk**, then write an audit-trail entry to  

       /home/user/audit/policy_checksum.log  

    3. The log file must contain exactly **one** line, terminated by a single
       newline character, and follow this rigid three-column format (columns
       separated by **two consecutive space characters**):

       TIMESTAMP␠␠ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_FILE␠␠SHA256_CHECKSUM

       • TIMESTAMP: the literal text 2023-09-01T00:00:00Z  
       • ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_FILE: /home/user/documents/policy.txt  
       • SHA256_CHECKSUM: the SHA-256 digest you just computed

       Example (spacing shown with “␠” for clarity):

       2023-09-01T00:00:00Z␠␠/home/user/documents/policy.txt␠␠<computed-sha256>\n

       There must be **no leading or trailing spaces**, and **two** spaces
       between the three fields.

    4. If /home/user/audit does not yet exist, create it with default user
       permissions.

    5. Do everything with normal user privileges; do not request or use sudo.

    When you are finished, the file /home/user/audit/policy_checksum.log should
    exist exactly as described so that an automated audit script can parse it
    without error.
truth:
<!--
      Pre-existing data
    -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/documents/policy.txt">
abc</initial_file>
    <!--  note: the string 'abc' has no trailing newline -->

    <!--
      The correct SHA-256 checksum of the above exact contents (‘abc’ without a
      trailing newline) is well-known:
    -->
    <expected_checksum>ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad</expected_checksum>

    <!--
      The audit log produced by a correct solution must therefore be:
    -->
    <expected_log path="/home/user/audit/policy_checksum.log">
2023-09-01T00:00:00Z  /home/user/documents/policy.txt  ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad
</expected_log>
name: task_000000_db76c8d9
